content.full_text:
February 2, 1978


Arthur Gray, Treasurer
Thorn Re-Election Committee
314 W. Lincoln
Anaheim, California 92805

Dear Mr. Gray:

This letter is in response to our telephone conversation on January
24, 1978 regarding the disposition of surplus funds by a committee.

You have inquired whether there are any restrictions on how surplus
funds of a committee can be disposed of. The Political Reform Act
does not place any restriction on the disposition of surplus funds.
I urge you, however, to contact the appropriate local officials in
your area and inquire if there are any local ordinances that might
restrict the disposition of surplus funds.

As I am sure you are aware, included in the Statement of Organization
filed by you on behalf of the committee, there was a description of
the disposition of surplus funds in the event of termination. When
the surplus funds are actually disbursed, in accordance with the
description in the Statement of Organization or any amendments thereto, the payment, since it will be made by a controlled committee,
is an expenditure, pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18225(b)(2),
which must be reported on a campaign statement filed by the Thorn
Re-Election Committee. ("Committee").

Finally, I am enclosing, for your possible use, Form 415 (Recipient
Committee Statement of Termination) which may be filed by the committee after it files a campaign statement disclosing all reportable
transactions, including the disbursement of the surplus funds. For
your information, I am also enclosing a copy of the Fair Political
Practices Commission's regulation on termination of committees,
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 13404.

If I can be of further assistance, do not hesitate to contact me.

Very truly yours,


Alan Herndon
Compliance Representative

Enclosure

(Illustrative Form)

COMMITTEE
STATEMENT OF ORGANIZATION

(GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION #1201-#1103)

RECEIVED
AND FILED
In the office of the Secretary of State
of the State of California

FEB 13 1975

MARCH FONG EU, Secretary of State

Form 410

FULL NAME OF COMMITTEE

THOM REELECTION COMMITTEE

STREET ADDRESS (No. & STREET)          (NO. & STREET)

914 W. Lincoln             A Anaheim         □ Cal.         ZIP CODE
                                                              92805

AREA CODE | PHONE NO.          DATE ORGANIZED OR FORMED                FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY

                                Feb. 28, 1974    (714) 535-1157

I    TREASURER AND OTHER PRINCIPAL OFFICERS

POSITION                    NAME AND ADDRESS                           (AREA    PHONE
                                                                       CODE)    NO.

PRESIDENT and
TREASURER                   ARTHUR W. GRAY, JR., 914 W. Lincoln, Anaheim, Ca. 714  535-1157

VICE PRESIDENT              JOSEPH WHITE, 913 W. Lincoln, Anaheim, Ca.      714  774-9462

SECRETARY                   SALLY WHITE, 918 W. Lincoln, Anaheim, Ca.       714  774-9462


Attach additional information on appropriately labeled continuation sheets.

II   IS THIS A CONTROLLED COMMITTEE?

(A controlled committee is one which is controlled directly or indirectly by a candidate or which acts jointly with a
candidate or controlled committee in connection with the making of expenditures. A candidate controls a committee,
if he, his agent or any other committee he controls, has significant influence on the actions or decisions of the
committee)

[x] YES (Complete Section III)          □ NO (Section III is not applicable)

III  CANDIDATES AND COMMITTEES BY WHICH THIS COMMITTEE IS CONTROLLED OR WITH WHICH IT ACTS JOINTLY

FOR OFFICIAL                                                               IDENTIFICATION NO.
USE ONLY                 NAME OF CANDIDATE OR COMMITTEE                    (If Committee)

                         WILLIAM J. THOM


Attach additional information on appropriately labeled continuation sheets.

IV   ORGANIZATIONS, IF ANY, WITH WHICH THIS COMMITTEE IS AFFILIATED

                         NAME AND ADDRESS                                  (AREA     PHONE
                                                                          CODE)     NO.

                         NONE


Attach additional information on appropriately labeled continuation sheets.

YOU MUST COMPLETE THE VERIFICATION ON PAGE 2

State Measure □

THOM REELECTION COMMITTEE

CANDIDATES SUPPORTED

FULL NAME                                          OFFICE SOUGHT

WILLIAM J. THOM                                    Anaheim City Counc




Each additional information on appropriately labeled continuation sheets.

VI MEASURES SUPPORTED OR OPPOSED

TITLE                                    No. of    CHECK ONE
                                        Letter
NONE                                              ☐ Support  ☐ Oppose

                                                  ☐ Support  ☐ Oppose

                                                  ☐ Support  ☐ Oppose

Each additional information on appropriately labeled continuation sheets.

VII IN THE EVENT OF DISSOLUTION, WHAT DISPOSITION WILL BE MADE OF SURPLUS FUNDS?

Not determined, but such disposition would be made (if any surplus

arose) in such manner as would be permitted by law.

VERIFICATION

I declare under penalty of perjury that to the best of my knowledge this statement is true, correct and complete and that I have
used all reasonable diligence in its preparation.


Executed on Feb. 7, 1975     at  Anaheim, Ca.     by  [signature]
              (DATE)              (CITY AND STATE)        SIGNATURE OF TREASURER

                                                          Arthur W. Gray, Jr.
